concerns.analysis: I have found an instance of a missing hyperlink. On page 6 of the source PDF, a long sentence starting with 'support the Carolina heelsplitter...' is fully hyperlinked with a 'mailto:' address. In the output JSON, this sentence appears as plain text within its paragraph, and the hyperlink is missing from the correct location. An unrelated, malformed link object with garbled text was created at the end of the page content, but the original, correct link is gone. This constitutes a critical defect
concerns.source_pdf_quote: On page 6, the sentence 'one of only three watersheds in North Carolina to still support the Carolina heelsplitter (Lasmigona decorata), a federally and state endangered freshwater mussel.' is a clickable hyperlink to 'mailto:aeckardt@wildlandseng.com'.
concerns.output_quote: The sentence is present as plain, un-hyperlinked text in the paragraph. A malformed link object `{ "type": "link", "text": "support the Carolina heelsplit mussel ” According to the rep", "url": "mailto:aeckardt@wildlandseng.com" }` appears at the end of the page's content array.
concerns.location: Page 6, in the paragraph starting 'The project is located within the Yadkin River Hydrologic Unit Code...'

concerns.analysis: The HTML output is missing four critical, meaningful maps that are present in the source PDF. These are Figure 1 (Project Vicinity Map), Figure 2 (Project Service Area Map), Figure 3 (Project Component/Asset Map), and Figure 4 (Current Condition Plan View Map). The image manifest confirms that the image data for these was not extracted (b64_len: 0). These maps contain essential spatial information that is not replicated in the text, and their absence constitutes a critical loss of information fo
concerns.source_pdf_quote: The source PDF contains four maps: "Figure 1. Project Vicinity Map" (p. 13), "Figure 2. Project Service Area Map" (p. 14), "Figure 3. Project Component/Asset Map" (p. 15), and "Figure 4. Current Condition Plan View Map" (p. 20).
concerns.output_quote: The HTML output does not contain the image data for these four maps. Instead, it shows text placeholders like `[Image: A project vicinity map for the Running Dog Mitigation Site...]`. The image manifest confirms this with `"b64_len": 0` for each of these images.

concerns.description:
Header/footer elements retained in output (26): Wildlands Engineering, Inc. • phone 704-332-7754 • fax 704-3; Wildlands Engineering, Inc. • phone 704-332-7754 • fax 704-3; Running Dog Mitigation Site
Monitoring Year 1 Annual Report
concerns.analysis: header_footer_count=26, pages_affected=20/42. Header/footer text from extraction JSON is present in the rendered HTML across most pages — pervasive pipeline failure. Screen readers will encounter duplicate artifacts on every page.

On page 5, the Table of Contents for the Appendices section is converted into a 2-column table. The source document clearly shows a hierarchical structure where items like 'Figure 1' and 'Table 1' belong under 'Appendix 1', and items like 'Site Overview Photographs' belong under 'Appendix 2'. The output table flattens this hierarchy. For sub-items like 'Site Overview Photographs', it creates a table row with an empty cell in the first column and the text in the second. This structurally fails to
concerns.source_pdf_quote: The Table of Contents on page 'i' (PDF page 5), under the 'APPENDICES' heading, shows several items listed and indented under 'Appendix 2', including 'Site Overview Photographs' and 'Vegetation Plot Photographs'.
concerns.output_quote: The JSON for the table on page 5 contains rows like: `{"text": "", "column_start": 0, "row_start": 12, ...}, {"text": "Site Overview Photographs", "column_start": 1, "row_start": 12, ...}`. The empty cell in the first column indicates a structural misinterpretation of the nested source content.
concerns.location: Page 5, in the `table` element that represents the Appendices section of the Table of Contents.

concerns.analysis:
The Table of Contents on page 5 of the source document contains a clear, multi-level numbered list (e.g., '1.1', '1.2', '4.1', '4.2', '4.3', etc.). The conversion pipeline failed to identify this as a list and instead rendered each list item as a separate paragraph element. For instance, '1.1 Project Description...' is output as a `

` tag containing the text '1.1 ...' rather than a `

  • ` element within a semantic list structure. This is a failure of pattern (4) of the S5 check, as numbered it
concerns.source_pdf_quote: The Table of Contents on page 'i' (PDF page 5), showing numbered and indented items such as '1.1 Project Description', '1.2 Project Goals and Objectives', and '4.1 Vegetation Success Criteria and Monitoring Protocol'.
concerns.location: Page 5, affecting the series of paragraph elements from 'Section 1' through 'Section 6'.

concerns.analysis: The heading hierarchy is generally well-preserved. However, on page 41, the text 'Stephanie Erickson', which is part of an email header, has been incorrectly classified as a level 1 heading (H1). This text is not a structural heading for the document or a section. Misclassifying it as an H1 disrupts the semantic structure and would be confusing for a screen reader user.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Text 'Stephanie Erickson' at the top of the email on page 41.

concerns.analysis: The document contains several pages of photographs (pp. 23, 24, 25, 27, 28). In the source PDF, each page displays 4 or 6 unique photos. The HTML output incorrectly extracts composite images (e.g., an entire column of photos) and then duplicates these composite images for each individual photo's caption. The image manifest confirms this, showing only two unique image sizes being repeated for all photos on a given page. This results in the same group of photos being displayed multiple times on th
concerns.source_pdf_quote: Page 23 of the PDF contains 6 unique photographs, individually captioned: PP1 Upstream, PP1 Downstream, PP2 Upstream, PP2 Downstream, PP3 Upstream, and PP3 Downstream.
concerns.output_quote: The HTML contains 6 `
concerns.location: Image galleries starting on page 23, "SITE OVERVIEW PHOTOGRAPHS". This pattern of duplication repeats on pages 24, 25, 27, and 28.

concerns.description: 2 bare URL(s) in text not converted to hyperlinks: http://cvs.bio.unc.edu/protocol/cvs-eep-protocol-v4.2-lev1-2.pdf; https://files.nc.gov/ncdeq/Mitigation%20Services/Watershed_Planning/Yadkin_River
concerns.analysis: bare_url_count=2. URLs appear as plain text instead of clickable links. Per NCDIT criteria, link text not wrapped in href is a major defect.

The source PDF uses underlining for emphasis in several places, which is not consistently preserved in the output. For instance, the name in the signature block on page 4 is underlined in the source but is rendered as plain text in the output. Similarly, URLs in the reference section on page 11 are underlined in the source but appear as non-underlined text in the output. This constitutes a loss of visual emphasis.
concerns.source_pdf_quote: On page 4, the name "Andrea S. Eckardt" is underlined. On page 11, the URL "http://cvs.bio.unc.edu/protocol/cvs-eep-protocol-v4.2-lev1-2.pdf" is underlined.
concerns.output_quote: On page 4, the JSON output is `{"type": "paragraph", "text": "Andrea S. Eckardt\nEcological Assessment Team Leader"}` with no indication of an underline. On page 11, the URL is part of a paragraph with no underline: `"text": "Lee, Michael T., ... Retrieved from: http://cvs.bio.unc.edu/protocol/cvs-eep-protocol-v4.2-lev1-2.pdf"}`.
concerns.location: Page 4, signature block and Page 11, Section 6: REFERENCES.

concerns.description: Repeated footer/header text (2x): "1430 s. mint st, suite 104"
concerns.analysis: Short text with footer-like pattern appears on 2 pages

concerns.description: Displaced hyperlinks: 2 link(s) extracted as standalone elements instead of inline text
concerns.analysis: standalone_links=2, examples=['mailto:aeckardt@wildlandseng.com', 'mailto:aeckardt@wildlandseng.com']. Links should be inline within paragraphs, not separate blocks.

concerns.description: Table header markup issues: 7 table(s) with 3+ rows have no <th> header cells
concerns.analysis: data_in_th_time=0, no_th_tables=7. Minor semantic markup issues: tables lack <th> elements or use time values as headers. These affect screen reader context but are less severe than data misrepresentation.
